title: Weak OTP or Missing MFA Step for OT VPN

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval mfa=coalesce(factor_type, auth_method, mfa_mechanism)
| where stage="login" AND (isnull(mfa) OR mfa="none" OR mfa="password-only")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT requires_mfa
| where requires_mfa="true"
| stats count by user, portal, public_ip, host
| sort - count
value: Identifies VPN authentications that skip second factor or reuse legacy token methods for paths into OT jump zones.
implementation: (1) Map portals to MFA policy; (2) upgrade GP app versions enforcing MFA; (3) block legacy profiles; (4) weekly exception report to IAM.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.dest | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
